findings.evidence: `rg -n "timeSlice_.*(conj|sum|quad|norm|bound|finite)|timeSlice_isPositiveDefiniteKernel"` finds only `timeSlice_isPositiveDefiniteKernel` at line 59, origin facts, `timeSlice_isPositiveDefinite`, and the continuity package; the generic APIs exist as `isPositiveDefiniteKernel_iff`, `isPositiveDefiniteKernel_conj_symm`, and `isPositiveDefiniteKernel_normSq_le`.
findings.fix: Add named slice wrappers, with `@[simp]` on the conjugate-symmetry normal form and a `timeSlice_normSq_le`/boundedness lemma reusing `isPositiveDefiniteKernel_normSq_le` (importing `KernelBounds` if needed).
findings.issue: `timeSlice_isPositiveDefiniteKernel` exports only the predicate; this file has no fixed-time elimination/normal-form lemmas such as finite quadratic-form nonnegativity, `conj (F (t, v - w)) = F (t, w - v)`, or the Cauchy--Schwarz bound for the slice.

summary: The slice kernel itself is exposed, but the characteristic public API for using that slice is incomplete. Downstream Bochner work should not have to reopen the generic kernel predicate just to recover the finite form, symmetry, and bounds of the fixed-time spatial kernel.
